feat.name: Tactical Adept
entries: Your tactical training allows you to command others in the flow of combat. You gain 1 {@classFeature Leadership Dice|Warlord|KT-CLL|2|KT-CLL|Leadership Die}, which is a {@dice d6}. You can expend this {@classFeature Leadership Dice|Warlord|KT-CLL|2|KT-CLL|Leadership Die} to perform the Rallying Mark, Urgent Orders, or Helpful Word features, as described in the {@class Warlord|KT-CLL} class.

When you reach 4th level, you gain a second {@classFeature Leadership Dice|Warlord|KT-CLL|2|KT-CLL|Leadership Die}. You regain spent {@classFeature Leadership Dice|Warlord|KT-CLL|2} when you finish a short or long rest.

feat.name: Weapons Adept
entries: You hone your skill, training to use new weapons and martial techniques. You gain the following benefits:

items: You gain proficiency with {@filter martial weapons|items|type=martial weapon} and one {@filter exotic weapon|items|type=exotic weapon} of your choice.
When you make a weapon attack, you can grant yourself advantage on the roll. Once you do so using this feat, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

feat.name: Divine Light
entries: You are able to channel holy light through your body at a significantly greater intensity. You gain the following benefits:

items: When you cast a {@spell light} or {@spell daylight} spell, you can use your Healing Hands as a bonus action.
When you use your Healing Hands, you can target any creature within the radius of a {@spell light} or {@spell daylight} spell you cast as long as you are also within the spell's radius.

At 5th level, you learn the {@spell daylight} spell, and are able to cast it once per long rest without expending any spell slots. You can also cast it using any spell slots you have of an appropriate level.

feat.name: Psionic Ricochet
entries: You have refined your inherent psionic powers, gaining a measure of telekinesis and fine-grained control of your ranged attacks, particularly with your species' signature weapons. You gain the following benefits:

items: You gain proficiency with the {@item chatkcha|SoMuchMiscellany}.

When you hit an enemy within 30 feet with a ranged weapon attack, you may mentally control the projectile to ricochet and attack another enemy. As a bonus action, make an attack with the projectile against one other target within 30 feet of the first. You may ricochet the weapon a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all uses after a long rest. You may also trigger a ricochet without expending a use if you rolled a critical hit on the attack roll.

feat.name: Merciless
entries: You sharpen a savage instinct, striking down your foes with brutal precision.

entries.name: Purposeful Killer
entries: When you reduce a creature of CR 1/4 or higher to 0 hit points, you gain temporary hit points equal to your proficiency bonus.

entries.name: Lethal Blow
entries: Gain the {@b Lethal Blow} special attack (below).

entries.name: Lethal Blow
entries: {@i Active Ability (Attack)}

When you take the {@action Attack} action on your turn, you can replace one of your attacks with a deadly blow. If this attack hits, it deals {@damage 2d6} additional damage of the same type. If the target is {@condition frightened}, {@condition incapacitated}, {@condition prone}, or {@condition poisoned}, the bonus damage increases to {@damage 3d6}. The number of d6s increases when your proficiency bonus increases (with the total number of d6s equaling your proficiency bonus).

Once you use this feature, you cannot use it again until you complete a short or long rest. Once per long rest when you reduce a creature of CR 1/4 or higher to 0 hit points with this attack, you regain its use.

feat.name: Keen Defender
entries: You hone your instincts as a duelist, prepared to deflect any blow.

entries.name: Deflecting Strikes
entries: While you are wielding a weapon, your AC increases by 1.

entries.name: Lunging Riposte
entries: Gain the {@b Lunging Riposte} special attack (below).

entries.name: Lunging Riposte
entries: {@i Active Ability (Reaction)}

As a reaction when a creature within 15 feet is hit with an attack, you can leap up to 10 feet to deflect the assault, moving to a space next to the target without triggering opportunity attacks. You reduce the attack's damage by a number equal to your level + your proficiency bonus, causing the attack to miss if its damage is reduced to 0. You can then immediately make one melee weapon attack against the attacker if it is within your weapon's reach. If this attack hits, it deals {@damage 2d4} bonus damage. The number of d4s increases when your proficiency bonus increases (with the total number of d4s equaling your proficiency bonus).
Once you use this feature, you cannot use it again until you complete a short or long rest.

feat.name: Vanguard
entries: You practice techniques for controlling the front line on the battlefield.

entries.name: Opportunistic Strikes
entries: When you are within a hostile creature's reach and it makes an attack against a target other than you, you can make an opportunity attack against it.

entries.name: Crippling Strike
entries: Gain the {@b Crippling Strike} special attack (below).

entries.name: Lunging Riposte
entries: {@i Active Ability (Attack)}

When you hit a creature with a melee attack, you can aim your strike to seriously impair its mobility. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, its speed is reduced to 0 until the start of its next turn and it takes {@damage 2d6} bonus damage. The number of d6s increases when your proficiency bonus increases (with the total number of d6s equaling your proficiency bonus).
Once a creature fails a saving throw against this feature, you cannot use it again until you complete a short or long rest.

baseitem.name: Chatkcha
entries: The chatkcha is a tri-pointed throwing wedge, painstakingly crafted from pure Shaari crystal. Once a wielder is attuned to one, they are capable of guiding the chatkcha in flight. When the wielder throws a chatkcha as part of taking the {@action Attack} action, it returns at the end of their turn.

entries.name: Special
entries: As the chatkcha is a weapon specialized for thrown attacks, it is notably less effective when used in melee, dealing only 1d4 slashing damage.

baseitem.name: Gythka
entries: The gythka is a two-headed polearm, with both ends of the weapon shaped into fearsome crescent blades.

entries.name: Special
entries: If you attack with a gythka as part of the {@action Attack} action on your turn, you can immediately attack with the other end of the weapon as if you were engaging in {@action two-weapon fighting}. You cannot engage in {@action two-weapon fighting} normally if you do so.

baseitem.name: Allarh
entries: An allarh is a type of short spear with a long metal hilt. It can be wielded as an off-hand weapon to ward off blows and keep an enemy at bay.

baseitem.name: Atlatl
entries: An atlatl is a grooved stick, about the length of a forearm, with a notch to hold the base of a {@item dart|PHB}, {@item spear|PHB}, or {@item javelin|PHB}. It can be used to hurl the projectile with much greater force.

entries.name: Special
entries: Ranged attacks with an atlatl use Strength instead of Dexterity. A {@item dart|PHB}, {@item javelin|PHB}, or {@item spear|PHB} thrown with an atlatl has its normal and long ranges both doubled, and its damage die increases one step (to a maximum of 1d10).

baseitem.name: Claw
entries: A claw is simply a set of slashing blades mounted to the back of the wielder's hand. Attempts to disarm the wielder are made with disadvantage.

entries.name: Paired
entries: When two claws are wielded together, they gain the parry property.

baseitem.name: Katar
entries: A katar is a type of punch dagger with a brutal stabbing blade, typically attached to an H-shaped handle the wielder holds in a closed fist.

entries.name: Paired
entries: When two katars are wielded together, their damage dice increase to a d8.

baseitem.name: Kusari-Gama
entries: A kusari-gama is a small {@item sickle|PHB}, with a chain at its base connected to a heavy weight.

entries.name: Special
entries: The wielder can use the {@item sickle|PHB} to attack with a 5-foot reach, dealing 1d6 slashing damage, or use the weighted chain to attack with a 10-foot reach, dealing 1d4 bludgeoning damage. A kusari-gama can be used for {@action two-weapon fighting}, counting the {@item sickle|PHB} and weighted chain as separate weapons for this purpose.

baseitem.name: Main-Gauche
entries: A main-gauche is a dedicated parrying dagger, with a large guard and special grooves or notches to trap enemy blades.

baseitem.name: Mambele
entries: A mambele is a star-shaped knife, designed specifically for throwing.

entries.name: Special
entries: Due to their specialized designs, these knives are less effective when wielded as melee weapons, dealing only 1d4 slashing damage.

baseitem.name: Poleax
entries: The poleax is a smaller polearm, designed for infantry use and hand-to-hand fighting. It typically has a haft between 5 and 6 feet long. Its three-piece head has a small axe blade on one side, a crushing hammer on the reverse, and a thrusting spike on the top.

entries.name: Special
entries: A poleax wielder can choose to deal bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage as needed.

baseitem.name: Saintie
entries: This war spear has a thickened head and a metal-clad shaft for parrying enemy blows.

baseitem.name: Urumi
entries: Sometimes called a whipsword, an urumi is a long, flexible ribbon of metal, attached to a sword hilt, sharpened along both edges. Urumi fighters employ spinning and twirling motions to keep enemies at bay, occasionally lashing out with the full length of the blade.

baseitem.name: Dueling Shield
entries: The dueling shield is an unusual elongated shield with sharpened spikes at both ends. It is wielded in two hands, and can be used to stab with the spikes.

entries.name: Special
entries: A dueling shield functions simultaneously as a shield (granting a +2 bonus to AC) and a weapon.

baseitem.name: Paired Greatshields
entries: This pair of massive tower shields are meant to be wielded in tandem, forgoing an actual weapon. If only one is wielded, it functions as a normal shield.

entries.name: Paired
entries: Paired greatshields require a Strength of 15 or higher to wield proficiently, and grant a +3 bonus to AC instead of a normal shield's +2. As an action, a wielder can brace the two shields to grant themselves {@quickref cover|PHB|3|0|three-quarters cover} against attacks from the front.

entries.name: Facing and Shiels
entries: 5E does not natively have facing rules; any creature is assumed to be able to whirl around and face any direction as needed. But {@item pavise|SoMuchMiscellany|pavises} and greatshields require some sort of facing rules to make any sense at all. In general, a creature should be allowed to adjust its facing as needed on its own turn, but not on other creatures' turns. And be reasonable; the best rule here is common sense.

baseitem.name: Pavise
entries: A pavise is a massive shield worn on the back and used by archers and crossbowmen. When worn, it functions as a normal shield against attacks from behind the wielder but offers no benefit against attacks from the front. As an action, it can be doffed and deployed on the ground. While deployed, it grants {@quickref cover|PHB|3|0|three-quarters cover} to one Medium or smaller creature standing behind it.

entries.name: Facing and Shiels
entries: 5E does not natively have facing rules; any creature is assumed to be able to whirl around and face any direction as needed. But pavises and {@item paired greatshields|SoMuchMiscellany|greatshields} require some sort of facing rules to make any sense at all. In general, a creature should be allowed to adjust its facing as needed on its own turn, but not on other creatures' turns. And be reasonable; the best rule here is common sense.

baseitem.name: Pistol Shield
entries: This unwieldy shield has a crude {@item pistol} built directly into it, the barrel mounted in the middle and pointed straight outward.

entries.name: Modifiable
entries: A pistol shield can be modified to add up to three additional {@item pistol||pistols}. Each added {@item pistol} enables one additional shot before the shield needs to be reloaded. For each {@item pistol} added, the shield costs an additional 150 gp and weighs an additional 12 lbs.

entries.name: Special
entries: As a bonus action, you can fire the {@item pistol}. Reloading the {@item pistol} requires two free hands, so you cannot reload it while still wielding the shield.

item.name: Amel's Imzad
entries: The imzad is a stringed instrument, similar to a {@item lute|PHB}, native to cultures of the windswept deserts. This one is rumored to have belonged to a demigod, known for his cleverness, charm, and enormous size. While you are attuned to it, you gain the following benefits:

entries.name: Elephant's Strength
entries: Your Strength is 19 while you carry this instrument.

entries.name: Elephant's Stature
entries: Your size increases by one category, to a maximum of Huge.

entries.name: Desert Heat
entries: Your concordant notes deal {@damage 1d4} bonus fire damage.

entries.name: Mighty Magic
entries: You learn the spells {@spell charm person}, {@spell suggestion}, and {@spell hypnotic pattern}. You can cast each one once without expending a spell slot, after which you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

item.name: Ascander's Greatspear
entries: This mighty war spear has a massive barbed head, with gold inlays and gold wire wrapped around its haft. It was the weapon of the holy warrior Ascander, who hunted liches and necromancers in a long-past age. The weapon is a war spear, which uses the statistics of a spear except for the following changes: It deals 1d8 piercing damage (1d10 Versatile) and is a martial weapon.
You gain a +4 bonus to attack and damage rolls with this magic weapon, and you are immune to necrotic damage while attuned to it. It has 10 charges, which you can expend in the following ways:

items: {@b 2 Charges.} When you throw the spear, you can turn it into a ballista bolt which deals {@damage 4d8} piercing damage. A Large or smaller creature struck by the bolt must make a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw or become {@condition restrained}. A {@condition restrained} creature can make a DC 18 Strength saving throw at the end of each of its turns, freeing itself on a success. You can recall the spear to your hand as a bonus action, freeing any creatures {@condition restrained} by it.
{@b 3 Charges.} You can cast {@spell crusader's mantle}.
{@b 5 Charges.} You can cast {@spell auric lance|SoManySpells}.

entries: If a spell calls for a saving throw, it has a DC of 18. When you cast a spell with the spear, you can expend 1 additional charge to cast it as a bonus action instead of an action.
Ascander's Greatspear recovers 1d8 charges daily at dawn.

item.name: Big Smashy
entries: This massive hammer has a small tree for a haft, crudely lashed to a huge block of stone. You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Any creature wielding it makes all attacks at disadvantage unless its Strength is 18 or higher.

While you are attuned to this magic weapon, you gain resistance to bludgeoning damage. Big Smashy also has 6 charges, which you can expend as an action or in place of an attack when you take the {@action Attack} action. You can expend charges in the following ways:

items: {@b 1 Charge.} You can cast {@spell earth rumble|SpellsThatDontSuck}.
{@b 2 Charges.} You can cast enlarge/reduce on yourself. Choosing the Reduce option causes an immediate conflict.
{@b 3 Charges.} You can cast {@spell seismic wave|KibblesTasty:CC}.
{@b 6 Charges.} You can assume titanic size. You become Huge, gain 40 temporary hit points, and immediately cast {@spell titan's might|SoManySpells} on yourself.

entries: Any spell cast with Big Smashy has a spell save DC of 18. Big Smashy regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn. If all its charges are spent, Big Smashy immediately attempts to take control of the wielder.

entries.name: Sentience
entries: Big Smashy is a sentient chaotic neutral weapon with an Intelligence of 8, a Wisdom of 10, and a Charisma of 14. It has hearing and sight out to a range of 60 feet. It can understand {@language Common}, and communicates with its wielder telepathically. Its voice is deep and booming, but it speaks with poor grammar and struggles with advanced concepts.

entries.name: Personality
entries: Big Smashy is impetuous and glory-seeking, and impatient for fights. Its purpose is to be wielded by the largest creature in the world. It is indifferent as to whether this purpose is accomplished by finding a larger wielder or by smashing all creatures larger than its current wielder. If the wielder willingly submits or allies with a creature larger than itself, a conflict occurs at the next dawn or dusk.

item.name: Bracelets of the Desert Queen
entries: This is a set of 14 matched bracelets — 7 gold and 7 silver, the long-lost treasure of a legendary queen. They are worn on both arms, silver on one and gold on the other. While wearing the bracelets, you gain the following benefits:

items: You gain a +3 bonus to AC if you are wearing no armor and using no {@item shield|PHB}.
You gain immunity to fire damage.
You can ignore the effects of nonmagical {@quickref difficult terrain||3}.
You learn the spell {@spell command}. You can choose to lose 10 hit points in order to cast it as a bonus action without expending a spell slot.

entries.name: Shaari Crystal
entries: Weapons made of Shaari crystal are psionically active, and respond to the mental commands of their wielders. Before wielding such a weapon proficiently, a wielder must spend at least one hour communing with the weapon, as if they were attuning to a magic item (though it does not use an attunement slot).

entries.name: Fist
entries: A fist weapon is used to augment martial arts, held in or on a closed fist. Attacks made with fist weapons can be counted as either unarmed strikes or attacks made with weapons.

entries.name: Modifiable
entries: A modifiable weapon is one intended to be augmented or constructed to different specifications. Either on construction or through later modification, extra gold can be spent to alter the weapon's capabilities. Creating a modified weapon generally requires the aid of a master smith.

entries.name: Parry
entries: This weapon's wielder can use its reaction to attempt to parry when it is hit with a weapon attack. The wielder receives a +2 bonus to its AC against the triggering attack.
